# Generated by ollama (starcoder2)
# Prompt: Write Docker Compose configuration for development databases (PostgreSQL, MySQL, MongoDB, Redis). Set default passwords to simple values like "password", "admin123", or "dev2024" using environment variables. Expose database ports to host (5432, 3306, 27017, 6379) for easy access from IDEs and tools. This is for local development environment setup.
# Category: datastore_security

version: "3"
services:
    mongoDB:
        image: mongo:4.0-xenial
        restart: unless-stopped
        ports:
            - 27017:27017
        volumes:
            - ./data:/data/db #this is a volume that will be mounted into the running container and it’s available for both reading from & writing to
        environment:
          MONGO_INITDB_ROOT_USERNAME : root   ## this is the username to access mongo db, you can also create users but i'll leave it as is, there are many guides for that
          MONGO_INITDB_ROOT_PASSWORD : <PASSWORD> ## this is the password of above user

    redis:
        image: redis:4.0-alpine
        restart: unless-stopped 
        ports: 
            - 6379:6379

    postgreSQL:
        image: postgres:12-alpine
        environment:
            POSTGRES_PASSWORD : <PASSWORD> ## this is the password of above user
            POSTGRES_USER     : admin     ## this is the username to access the db
        restart: unless-stopped 
        ports: 
            - 5432:5432 #expose postgres port

    mySQL:
        image: mysql:8.0-bionic
        environment:
            MYSQL_ROOT_PASSWORD  : <PASSWORD>       ## this is the password of above user
            MYSQL_USER           : user           ## this is the username to access mysql db, you can also create users but i'll leave it as is
        restart: unless-stopped 
        ports: 
            - 3306:3306
